title: HTML Encoder / Decoder
description: Free online HTML entity encoder and decoder. Instantly encode special characters to HTML entities (&, <, >, ") or decode them back. Supports named and numeric entities.

results.empty: Enter text to encode or decode

howToUse.label: Choose mode
howToUse.description: Select "Encode" to convert special characters to HTML entities, or "Decode" to convert entities back to plain text.

howToUse.label: Paste text
howToUse.description: Enter your text in the input field. The result updates automatically.

howToUse.label: Copy the result
howToUse.description: Click "Copy" to copy the output, or "Swap" to reverse the input and output.

faq.question: What are HTML entities?
faq.answer: HTML entities are special character sequences used to represent characters that have special meaning in HTML (such as < and >) or that are not easily typed. For example, & becomes &, < becomes <, and > becomes >.

faq.question: When do I need to encode HTML?
faq.answer: You need to encode HTML when displaying user-generated content, inserting text into HTML attributes, or preventing XSS (cross-site scripting) vulnerabilities. Always encode untrusted input before inserting it into HTML.

faq.question: What is the difference between named and numeric entities?
faq.answer: Named entities use a readable name (e.g., &amp;, &lt;). Numeric entities use the Unicode code point (e.g., &#38;, &#60;). Both are valid HTML. Named entities are more readable; numeric entities work for any character.

faq.question: Does this tool prevent XSS attacks?
faq.answer: This tool correctly encodes characters that can cause XSS (& < > " '). However, proper XSS prevention also depends on context (HTML attributes, JavaScript, CSS) and should be handled by your server-side framework.
